title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following an inquest, Major Melrose drives Bondell back to the Chimney House while Jimmy returns to London. Disturbed by Bondell's account, Jimmy calls Lorna to inform her that the inquest ruled an incident an accident, though he suspects a cover-up. He warns her to be careful, prompting her to express concern for his own safety. Jimmy then instructs his associate, Stevens, to procure an automatic handgun with a blued barrel.

On Friday afternoon, Bondell attends a gala at the Two-Footed Dragon Church. George Lomax greets her warmly, noting her interest in politics. Jimmy meets Bondell there, appearing more serious than usual, and whispers to her that he has informed Bill about their "little scheme." Bondell expresses frustration with Bill's perceived lack of intelligence, though Jimmy defends him, noting Bill's physical strength.

During the event, George introduces Bondell to various guests, including Sir Stanley Digby, Mr. O'Roke, Mr. Osborne, Lady Cutt, Mr. Bettman, and the beautiful Countess Razky. Lady Cutt discusses a recent car accident with Jimmy, while Jimmy privately mocks the "gorilla" (referring to a guest) who is enchanted by the Countess. Sir Oswald Kuter discusses property with Bondell, expressing interest in the Chimney House, which leaves her feeling indignant. 

After changing her clothes, Bondell encounters a bulky man dressed like a butler. She identifies him as Detective Inspector Bartholomew, who has arrived in disguise to protect Mr. Lomax following a threatening letter. Bondell finds his disguise unconvincing, but the Inspector remains undeterred.
